title: Release manifest references a generic tarball URL that the build pipeline never produces
reasoning: generate-release-manifest.ts emits `tarball_url` pointing to `prism-v${version}.tar.gz` (no platform suffix, line 54). However, build-bundle.ts only produces platform-specific tarballs named `prism-v${version}-${platformKey}.tar.gz` (line 62). No step in the included scripts creates the generic (platform-less) tarball. If the update client falls back to `tarball_url` when no platform-specific bundle matches (e.g., an unsupported platform like win32 or freebsd), it will fetch a 404 URL. The manifest also emits `sha256_url` and `signature_url` for this non-existent file.
reproduction: Run the full release pipeline (build-bundle for all expected platforms, then generate-release-manifest). Attempt to download the URL in the manifest's top-level `tarball_url` field; it will 404 unless a separate, non-included step uploads that artifact.
recommendation: Either (a) add a build step that produces the generic source/fallback tarball referenced by `tarball_url`, or (b) remove the generic `tarball_url`/`sha256_url`/`signature_url` fields from the manifest and have clients use only the per-platform `bundles` map, failing explicitly when no matching platform exists.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests validate the release manifest schema against actual build artifacts. The included test suite (bench/**/*.test.ts) covers engine logic only, not release tooling.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a CI validation step (or unit test) that asserts every URL path in the generated manifest.json corresponds to an artifact that exists in the release output directory.
minimumFixScope: Either remove lines 54-55 and the corresponding manifest fields (tarball_url, sha256_url, signature_url) in generate-release-manifest.ts, or add a build step in build-bundle.ts that produces the generic tarball.
